guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Treeline has a clearer shelter/exposure read outside the High Pass route and no new High Pass density.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Ensure Treeline route/station states remain distinct from filed High Pass closure.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Refresh one Treeline relationship prompt or atlas line around krummholz, stone, wind, or lee shelter.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Tune a lower Treeline or shelter-line spatial beat, explicitly avoiding the High Pass opener/endcap bands.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Improve an existing Treeline route support/replay cue without creating another High Pass route.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

recommended_snapshot.reason: A debug-only loadable state after the coastal line but before High Pass lets reviewers prove the station/map/journal seam still presents ordinary Treeline Stone Shelter rather than filed-season High Pass closure.

expected_state: no route-marker, tundra-short-season, tundra-survey-slice, treeline-low-fell, forest-season-threads, High Pass, filed-season archive, save-schema, or station-shell change
expected_assertions: guidedFieldSeason.stage settled with nextBiomeId treeline and STONE SHELTER station note
world map current forest, focused treeline, and routeMarkerLocationId null
field station routes page keeps hand-lens / HAND LENS selected
route board remains TREELINE SHELTER LINE targeting treeline with treeline-shelter active and Stone Shelter summary
route board does not show a HIGH PASS launch card or filed High Pass copy
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "route board to treeline|High Pass"

lane_1_main_result.summary: Added the debug-only treeline-stone-shelter save snapshot and focused resolver/runtime assertions proving the ordinary Treeline Stone Shelter station, map, and journal seam stays distinct from High Pass closure.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "route board to treeline|High Pass"
direct Playwright treeline-stone-shelter snapshot proof with no console/page errors
scope_preserved: no player-facing station behavior, route definitions, lane-2 Treeline copy, support-choice behavior, world-map focus priority, save schema, Treeline geometry, High Pass copy, or route-controller behavior changed

lane_1_critic_result.summary: Confirmed the treeline-stone-shelter snapshot stays debug/test/docs only, round-trips as plain SaveState, and proves Stone Shelter station/map/journal state without High Pass launch or filed-copy drift.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "route board to treeline"
source inspection of snapshot builder, docs, packet result, and browser proof state
residual_notes: The broader High Pass runtime-smoke filter currently catches an unrelated dirty-tree rime-footing summary expectation mismatch outside the lane-1 snapshot files.
scope_preserved: no player-facing station behavior, route definitions, lane-2 Treeline copy, support-choice behavior, world-map focus priority, save schema, Treeline geometry, High Pass copy, or route-controller behavior changed

recommended_target.reason: This existing note already links krummholz, raised stone, and marmot shelter, so it can strengthen Treeline's shelter/exposure identity without touching Route v2, High Pass closure, or spatial bands.

recommended_copy.summary: Bent krummholz and raised stone break wind into lee pockets animals can use.
recommended_copy.observationPrompt: Where do stone and bent wood make a lee pocket?

lane_4_scout_refinement.focus: Add a behavior-neutral controller proof for the existing Treeline `Brief Bloom` support/replay cue below High Pass.
main_acceptance: hand-lens active `Brief Bloom` retargets from nearer `mountain-avens` to farther `moss-campion` in `lichen-fell`
the hand-lens `NOTEBOOK J` chip stays `Brief Bloom` / `support-biased`
`note-tabs` keeps nearest-inspect behavior on `mountain-avens`, does not prefer the active clue, and keeps the chip progress-facing as `2/4 clues` / `support-biased`
no route definition, filed-note, support ordering, save schema, station, world-map, route-marker, geometry, High Pass, science-copy, or new UI surface changes
baseline_verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-request-controller.test.ts -t "Brief Bloom|Rimed Pass|High Pass|non-hand-lens"

lane_4_main_result.summary: Added controller-level `Brief Bloom` support proof for `treeline-low-fell`, pairing hand-lens active-clue retargeting to `moss-campion` against `note-tabs` nearest-inspect behavior on `mountain-avens`.
coverage: active request stays `treeline-low-fell` / `Brief Bloom` with `2/4 clues`
`moss-campion` receives `LENS CLUE: fell bloom` and is preferred only by hand-lens
hand-lens selection sets `supportRetargetsInspect` and `supportPrefersActiveClue` true and shows `Brief Bloom` / `support-biased`
note-tabs selection stays on the nearer `mountain-avens`, keeps both support flags false, and shows `2/4 clues` / `support-biased`
